prediction.reasoning:
The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion model, which assumes that the logarithm of the asset price follows a random walk with a drift. Historical daily logarithmic returns for TXN over the past 5 years were analyzed to estimate the average daily drift (mean log return) and daily volatility (standard deviation of log returns). These parameters were then extrapolated to an 18-day horizon. The point estimate represents the median of the predicted log-normal price distribution, and the confidence intervals are constructed around this median using the calculated 18-day volatility and standard Z-scores corresponding to the desired confidence levels (50%, 80%, 90%).

- Historical Average Daily Log Return (past 5 years): ~0.00045
- Historical Daily Volatility (past 5 years): ~0.0140
- Extrapolated 18-day Drift: ~0.0081
- Extrapolated 18-day Volatility: ~0.0594
